steps.title: Explain full liquidity
steps.content: The notes define liquid atoms as disordered and able to move three-dimensionally. In chain melt, potassium's guest atoms show liquid-like disorder, but the solid host lattice confines them to one-dimensional movement.

steps.title: Reject incomplete explanations
steps.content: A describes the dual structure without explaining why it is not fully liquid. C contradicts the note that chain melt is stable and distinct. D treats disorder as the problem, even though disorder is liquid-like. The confinement by a solid lattice explains the missing three-dimensional movement. The answer is B.
